reviewer.displayName: VSBDev (accountable operator) — independent academic review agent

conflictOfInterest.details: This run performed only the academic review role against the frozen candidate 82ef7e209fc3d16273ac7bb1279905bdcde52fcc. It did not author, revise, adjudicate, commit, or push, did not read the authoring conversation, and did not load another EmbeddedKnowledge role skill. To assess whether revision 0.1.1 resolves the prior academic defects, the operator directed this run to read the superseded cycle-1 academic artifact (same role, commit 3df8a3d); the learning-design and accessibility-and-rights artifacts were deliberately not opened. All findings below were re-derived from the candidate itself. The accountable principal github:VSBDev also operates the authoring agent under the disclosed founding-stage single-maintainer allowance; provenance here is disclosed and attested, not verified.

git diff 11c5b8b..82ef7e2 over the whole pack (the complete 0.1.0 to 0.1.1 change set) against the four prior academic findings
git diff 82ef7e2..c3641a8 (PR 5 head) confirming no lesson-pack file changed after the candidate; only generated site/data artifacts differ

findings.finding: The prior blocking-adjacent major finding is resolved and independently re-verified. Revision 0.1.1 removes every reference to a lesson pack `PREM-LPP-001`; `grep -rn PREM-LPP-001` over the pack at candidate 82ef7e209fc3d16273ac7bb1279905bdcde52fcc returns nothing, and `git ls-tree HEAD lessons/` confirms only this pack and `README.md` exist. Both failure paths of the sole prerequisite check now route to the graph outcome `topic-orientation-map-and-pathways` (the declared prerequisite in `lesson.json`, present in `site/data/premed-graph.json`), the scene supplies an executable bounded restatement of the node/prerequisite-link distinction, and it adds a fresh Zeta-Epsilon-Delta chain for immediate re-attempt. The scene now states explicitly that neither route "depends on a lesson pack being published," which is truthful at this commit. No unverifiable assertion about nonexistent content remains.
findings.resolution: No change required; the prior major finding is resolved.

findings.finding: The revision applies the new fragile/unresolved qualifier to a record where the discriminating probe was in fact attempted. The Stage 1 stem says the learner "gives only a memorized slogan as explanation," so an explanation probe was administered and produced weak evidence; under the revised definition in `content/030-concept.md` a "weakly explained" response is a positive fragile criterion, whereas "unresolved" is reserved for the case where "a probe needed to justify secure has not been attempted." The revised feedback nevertheless labels the case "provisionally fragile/unresolved" and states that "the record lacks the explanation and variation needed to justify secure," which contradicts the stem it is answering. Only the variation probe is genuinely missing. Stage 2 (Juniper: "no explanation or variation") is the correct application, so the two adjacent stages now teach the qualifier from inconsistent evidential situations. The classification itself (fragile) is right in both stages, so the instructional argument is not defeated, but the newly introduced distinction is illustrated by a case that does not exhibit it.
findings.resolution: Either drop the unresolved qualifier from Stage 1 and justify plain fragile on the weak explanation plus the missing variation probe, or change the Stage 1 stem so no explanation is elicited at all, and reword "the record lacks the explanation and variation" to name only the probe that is actually absent.

findings.finding: The revised routing rule now turns on the term "insecure," which the lesson never defines. The scene uses it five times ("declaring Alpha insecure," "If Beta is insecure," "shown insecure," "remains insecure," "actually shows insecure") and `claim-map-dependency-route` was rewritten around "the earliest dependency actually shown insecure," yet the operational vocabulary established in `content/030-concept.md` and `glossary.json` is unfamiliar / fragile / secure. "Insecure" is presumably the complement (unfamiliar or fragile), and the Zeta-Epsilon-Delta worked answer treats a "relevant but inconsistent response" as insecure, which is consistent with that reading — but a learner must infer it. The risk is sharpened by the same revision's warning that "unresolved" "is not a fourth knowledge state": a novice meeting an undefined fifth word in the very next scene has no stated basis for deciding whether "insecure" is another state. `CONTENT-STANDARD.md` section 4.2 requires defining a term on first use and keeping one name per entity.
findings.resolution: Gloss "insecure" once on first use in `content/020-prerequisite-diagnostic.md` as shorthand for "classified unfamiliar or fragile — that is, not yet supported as secure," or replace the word with "not yet shown secure" throughout the scene and the claim statement.

findings.finding: The lesson's central term "secure" collides with a different, quantitative definition in one of its own declared sources without the collision being flagged. `references.json#source-premed-syllabus` is cited at locator "Sections 3.2, 4, 7, and 18," and section 3.2 of `course/PREMED-SYLLABUS.md` defines a diagnostic result as "Secure (at least 85%, no critical misconception)" at station level. This lesson defines secure as a qualitative node-level pattern — accurate independent responses, defensible reasoning, and success under bounded variation — and explicitly teaches that a total score cannot choose a route (`content/010-orientation.md`). Both usages are individually defensible and the orientation scene does exclude administering the full entry diagnostic, but a learner who consults the cited syllabus section meets the same word carrying a percentage threshold. The two definitions are not equivalent: an 85% station score would not by itself satisfy this lesson's pattern requirement.
findings.resolution: Add one sentence to `content/030-concept.md` or `glossary.json#term-secure-knowledge` distinguishing this lesson's node-level operational "secure" from the syllabus's station-level percentage-based "Secure" placement result, so the terminology carries a single explicit scope.

findings.finding: The three prior minor findings are resolved and were re-checked against the candidate rather than accepted from the revision note. (1) The routing criterion no longer names Alpha as the single defensible answer on evidence-free stems; it now directs probing Beta first, reopening Alpha only once Alpha is itself shown insecure, and re-probing Gamma if Beta is secure — consistent with both `claim-map-dependency-route` as rewritten and the "least support that still addresses the observed gap" rule in `content/030-concept.md`. (2) `term-fragile-knowledge` now carries the conservative-under-insufficient-evidence condition and states that an unattempted operation must not be treated as a failed one; `content/030-concept.md`, `content/090-synthesis.md`, and Stage 2 practice apply it consistently (Stage 1 is the exception recorded above). (3) `scene-synthesis` is now listed in `claim-pattern-over-snapshot.sceneIds` and named in the synthesis `{source-note}`, so the pattern requirement in the synthesis table is registered under a claim mapped to that scene.

Quantitative content recomputed independently at this candidate and confirmed correct. Item points sum to 25 (2 + 3 + 10 + 10); each rubric's criteria sum exactly to its item's points (2; 2+1=3; 3+3+2+2=10; 4+2+2+2=10); `maxSelections: 2` matches the two-key answer for `item-separate-confidence`; scene `estimatedMinutes` sum to exactly 120, matching `lesson.json.estimatedMinutes` and the graph node's `estimatedHours: 2`. The mastery gate (at least 20 of 25 overall, at least 6 of 10 on both open items, no rubric criterion at zero) is satisfiable and non-contradictory, and its 80% overall threshold is consistent with the syllabus section 7 "Mastered" gate. The text correctly frames the gate as a provisional authoring decision that is not evidence of delayed retention.

All four answer keys were re-derived from the revised operational definitions and remain correct under 0.1.1. A cue-dependent correct response whose table variation fails supports fragile, not secure or unfamiliar; varied accurate independent performance with low confidence supports provisional secure plus a calibration flag; and Cedar / Flint / Harbor map to unfamiliar / fragile / provisionally secure as keyed, with Flint's failed bounded variation making plain fragile (not fragile/unresolved) the right label. No item rewards a scientifically false shortcut: the familiarity-only and confidence-only distractors are explicitly keyed wrong, the open items require an uncertainty or next probe, and `item-build-baseline` forbids averaging the three nodes into a whole-person label.

The distinction introduced by this revision is taught and practised but never sampled as mastery evidence. Every assessed record supplies a completed discriminating probe, so no item requires the learner to withhold a secure label because the probe is missing rather than failed — the exact confusion the revision was made to prevent. `item-build-baseline` partially compensates by requiring "one uncertainty or next probe," and this is an alignment observation rather than an academic error, so it is recorded for the learning-design role rather than treated as a defect in the answer logic.
findings.resolution: Optional: swap one open-item stimulus node so that a discriminating probe is absent rather than failed, and credit the fragile/unresolved routing decision in `rubric-build-baseline`.

findings.finding: This claim is the weakest evidential link in the pack and is recorded here as a bounded observation, not a defect. It is declared `support: synthesis` with `confidence: high` while resting on a single source (`source-premed-syllabus`). The cited syllabus text directly supports one direction — confidence is logged before feedback and a high-confidence incorrect response triggers misconception remediation (line 161), with a final calibration review requiring that confidence track performance (line 144). The converse taught by the lesson (low confidence can accompany accurate independent performance) is a logical complement of "compare confidence with performance" (line 850) rather than an explicit source statement. Comparable synthesis claims in this pack carry `medium-high`, and `source-authoring-evidence` is not cited here although it is the pack's natural evidence base. The claim's own uncertainty field correctly limits it to contextual evidence that does not reveal the cause of an error.
findings.resolution: Optional: lower `confidence` to `medium-high` for consistency with the pack's other synthesis claims, or add `source-authoring-evidence` to `sourceIds` if it supports the calibration direction as taught.

findings.finding: Recorded as a disclosure observation for the adjudication role, not an academic defect. One declared author run ID, `/root/author_prem_lpp_002`, now carries two different material-instructions digests: `sha256:dd8929...` for revision cycle 2 in `lesson.json`, and `sha256:3dab8b...` for initial authoring, retained only in `ATTRIBUTION.md`. `ATTRIBUTION.md` explains the split and the revision history is honest about using academic-review findings as revision input, which does not convert the author run into a review under the founding-stage rules. The academic consequence is only that the single structured provenance field no longer maps one-to-one to one instruction set; whether that satisfies the disclosure requirements in `AGENTS.md` and `site/agent/quorum-policy.json#agentIndependence` is a governance question outside this role.
findings.resolution: No academic change required; flagged for adjudication.

findings.target: content/010-orientation.md#exclusions, claims.json uncertainty fields, references.json agentAccess
findings.finding: Epistemic boundary handling remains a strength under revision and was re-checked against the failure modes this role must catch. The lesson excludes administering the full entry diagnostic, establishing delayed retention, diagnosing a learning difference or health condition, awarding credit, and making admissions decisions; every classification is labelled provisional; and each claim records a scope and an uncertainty that stops short of psychometric validity, including the explicit statement that the three labels are not a universally validated taxonomy. All records are fictional or self-generated and the pack instructs learners not to enter private educational or health data. No statement could reasonably be read as medical advice, clinical competence, or professional authorization. Separately, all four sources are repository-owned, pinned to commit 7688279 (verified present), carry `agentAccess.status: checked-no-agent-restriction-found`, and are byte-identical between that pin and the candidate, so every cited locator resolves at review time and no `human-only` source was processed.

limitations: This is one academic review run against one frozen candidate commit. It is a single eligible vote and establishes neither quorum nor merge readiness; the accompanying GitHub submission is a Comment review, not an Approve.

The operator directed this run to assess whether revision 0.1.1 resolves the prior academic defects, so the superseded cycle-1 academic artifact at commit 3df8a3d was read. This departs from the strictest reading of the role skill's isolation clause and is disclosed here. The learning-design and accessibility-and-rights artifacts were not opened, and every finding above was re-derived from the candidate content rather than carried over.

Verification was limited to the four repository-owned sources declared in references.json; no external literature was retrieved, so the meta-analytic effect sizes recorded in research/CONTENT-AUTHORING-EVIDENCE-2026-07-19.md were checked for faithful use by this lesson, not re-verified against the underlying primary studies.

The subject matter is learning-diagnostic decision logic rather than a natural-science domain, so there were no chemical equations, unit conversions, or numeric derivations to recalculate; quantitative checking was confined to assessment scoring, rubric point sums, scene-time totals, and the graph estimatedHours cross-check.

AUTHORING-BRIEF.md was inspected only through the 0.1.0-to-0.1.1 diff while scoping the revision; the academic judgment rests on learner-visible content and the structured pack artifacts, not on the authoring rationale.

PR 5 head at review time is c3641a84cb3b23403d70f1737eb3935a44001a6b, ahead of the candidate. That difference was verified to touch only generated site/data artifacts, so no lesson-pack file changed after the candidate; this review targets 82ef7e209fc3d16273ac7bb1279905bdcde52fcc only.
Provenance recorded here is self-reported and attested by the accountable principal; nothing in this artifact proves that the declared provider, model, run identity, or instruction digest were used.
No claim is made about instructional effectiveness, learner outcomes, or accessibility conformance; those belong to the learning-design and accessibility-and-rights roles.
